title: INTERCULTURAL PSYCHIATRIC PROGRAM - 211info
text: OHSU INTERCULTURAL PSYCHIATRIC PROGRAM, PORTLAND INTERCULTURAL PSYCHIATRIC PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides outpatient care to refugees and immigrants. Mental health counselors who speak the same languages as their clients, work with program psychiatrists to treat adults, children, and adolescents who have been affected by post-traumatic stress disorder. Services include individual and group therapy, case management, and medication management. 2525 SW 1st Avenue Portland, OR 97201 Eligibility: Focuses on refugees, immigrants, and non-English speaking clients. Other populations will be transferred to another clinic. Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm, Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (503) 494-4222 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 494-4222 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call to schedule an appointment. Individuals may self-refer or be referred by a health care provider, social service agency, other community organization, family, or friends. FEES: Accepts OHP, private insurance and private pay. Sliding scale fees available. DOCUMENTS: Insurance information, if applicable LANGUAGES: Amharic, Arabic, Bosnian, Burmese, Cambodian, Farsi, Kurdish, Laotian, Mai Mai, Mien, Oromo, Russian, Somali, Spanish (Central and South American), Swahili, Vietnamese AREA SERVED: Clackamas, Multnomah, and Washington Counties SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Services: Adolescent/Youth Counseling * Immigration Issues Family Counseling * Immigration Issues Group Counseling * Immigration Issues Individual Counseling * Immigration Issues If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. OHSU INTERCULTURAL PSYCHIATRIC PROGRAM, PORTLAND
